initial_program:
% Dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% Compute thresholds
t = (N + 3) \ 4.                     % toprow threshold column
bottomThresh = #max{0, 1, t - 2}.   % bottom threshold (max(1, t2))

% Cells that must become 3 (condition true)
cond(R,C) :- R < t, C = t.
cond(R,C) :- R >= t, (R + t < N), C <= M - 2.
cond(R,C) :- (R + t >= N), (C >= 1), (C >= t - 2), (C <= M - 2).

% Allowed output colour for a cell
allowed(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,Color).           % keep original colour
allowed(R,C,3)   :- cond(R,C).                    % also allow colour 3 when condition holds

% Output definition
output(R,C,Color) :- allowed(R,C,Color), color(0..9).

% Exactly one output per cell
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(0..9) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Constraints
% When the cell must be 3, any other colour is forbidden
:- output(R,C,Color), not (Color = 3), cond(R,C).

% When the cell should not become 3, keep the original colour
:- output(R,C,3), not cond(R,C), not input(R,C,3).

#show output/3.

% Dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% Compute thresholds
t = (N + 3) \ 4.                     % toprow threshold column
% bottom threshold (max(1, t - 2)).

% Cells that must become 3 (condition true)
cond(R,C) :- R < t, C = t.
cond(R,C) :- R >= t, R + t < N, C <= M - 2.
cond(R,C) :- R + t >= N, C >= 1, C >= t - 2, C <= M - 2.

% Allowed output colour for a cell
allowed(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,Color).           % keep original colour
allowed(R,C,3)   :- cond(R,C).                    % also allow colour 3 when condition holds

% Output definition

output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% When the cell should not become 3, keep the original colour
:- output(R,C,3), not cond(R,C), not input(R,C,3).

% Dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% Compute thresholds
t = (N + 3) \ 4.                     % toprow threshold column
% bottom threshold (max(1, t - 2)).

% Cells that must become 3 (condition true)
cond(R,C) :- R < t, C = t.
cond(R,C) :- R >= t, R + t < N, C <= M - 2.
cond(R,C) :- R + t >= N, C >= 1, C >= t - 2, C <= M - 2.

% Allowed output colour for a cell
allowed(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,Color).           % keep original colour
allowed(R,C,3)   :- cond(R,C).                    % also allow colour 3 when condition holds

% Output definition
output(R,C,Color) :- allowed(R,C,Color), color(0..9).

% Exactly one output per cell
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(0..9) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Constraints
% When the cell must be 3, any other colour is forbidden
:- output(R,C,Color), not Color = 3, cond(R,C).

% When the cell should not become 3, keep the original colour
:- output(R,C,3), not cond(R,C), not input(R,C,3).

#show output/3.
